channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. men's soccer star Christian Pulisic will take to the field after injury
item.description: Christian Pulisic, the longtime star of the U.S. men's national soccer team, has been slowed by an injury this World Cup. He's finally healthy and ready for his big moment.
item.content:encoded: Christian Pulisic, the longtime star of the U.S. men's national soccer team, has been slowed by an injury this World Cup. He's finally healthy and ready for his big moment.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: This historian wants to shatter the myth of the Old West
item.description: Megan Kate Nelson, author of The Westerners wants to shatter the myth of the frontier, one of white easterners coming west in covered wagons with a nuclear family.
item.content:encoded: Megan Kate Nelson, author of The Westerners wants to shatter the myth of the frontier, one of white easterners coming west in covered wagons with a nuclear family.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Warner

item.title: Another blockbuster Supreme Court term in review
item.description: The Supreme Court's ruling that presidents can fire leaders of independent agencies — at will — could lead to less regulation of corporate interests.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court's ruling that presidents can fire leaders of independent agencies — at will — could lead to less regulation of corporate interests.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: In their seventh film, the minions make mayhem on the silver screen
item.description: Everybody's favorite yellow, pill-shaped pranksters go Hollywood in the 1920s in their seventh big-screen adventure, Minions and Monsters.
item.content:encoded: Everybody's favorite yellow, pill-shaped pranksters go Hollywood in the 1920s in their seventh big-screen adventure, Minions and Monsters.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: If wars are so costly, why do we keep fighting them?
item.description: We dig into current conflicts to understand the incentives that lead countries into violence, and what keeps them stretching on and on.
item.content:encoded: We dig into current conflicts to understand the incentives that lead countries into violence, and what keeps them stretching on and on.
item.dc:creator: Darian Woods

item.title: Democratic socialists are on a winning streak. Here's what that means for Democrats
item.description: Last week, a pair of democratic socialists won competitive primary matchups in New York. Another in Philadelphia back in May, and on Tuesday in Denver, it happened again.
item.content:encoded: Last week, a pair of democratic socialists won competitive primary matchups in New York. Another in Philadelphia back in May, and on Tuesday in Denver, it happened again.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: North Carolina's mountainous hills become proving ground for downhill skateboarding
item.description: The mountainous hills of Western North Carolina are welcoming athletes of a growing and dangerous sport -- downhill skateboarding.
item.content:encoded: The mountainous hills of Western North Carolina are welcoming athletes of a growing and dangerous sport -- downhill skateboarding.
item.dc:creator: Laura Hackett

item.title: Lessons learned from a decades-old heat tragedy help Chicago and other cities
item.description: A heat dome has sent temperatures spiraling in rural and urban areas. Local governments have issued warnings and set up ways for residents to cool off in an effort to prevent heat-related deaths.
item.content:encoded: A heat dome has sent temperatures spiraling in rural and urban areas. Local governments have issued warnings and set up ways for residents to cool off in an effort to prevent heat-related deaths.
item.dc:creator: Alex Degman

item.title: Poll shows Americans have optimism about the U.S. improving despite concerns
item.description: Most Americans feel proud of their country, but at the same time many are worried about its future, a new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll shows.
item.content:encoded: Most Americans feel proud of their country, but at the same time many are worried about its future, a new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Maham Javaid

item.title: Victor Willis, former voice of The Village People and co-writer of 'Y.M.C.A.,' has died
item.description: Vocalist Victor Willis, the former lead singer of the disco band The Village people, has died. He was not only the front man but he also co authored several of the group's hits.
item.content:encoded: Vocalist Victor Willis, the former lead singer of the disco band The Village people, has died. He was not only the front man but he also co authored several of the group's hits.
item.dc:creator: Felix Contreras
